prompt: A gritty, revolutionary Soviet Constructivist propaganda poster, circa 1925. A flat, powerful palette of revolutionary red, deep black, and aged cream paper texture. Heavy lithographic print texture, ink bleed on all edges, and visible rough paper grain. Strictly no 3D shading, no gradients, no digital smooth finishes. The composition uses powerful, conflicting diagonal axes. The central image is a large, powerful, grainy black-and-white halftone photomontage cutout of a Soviet industrial engine assembly. This engine is completely immobilized and paralyzed. A thick, chaotic, erratic, jagged red wire web (representing the Overfit Line) is violently wrapped around the main gears, physically jamming the mechanism. This chaotic wire is trying to connect to dozens of scattered, numbered heavy iron geometric bolts and plates (the 'Noise' data points, labeled roughly with numbers like 3, 11, 13, 29) that are physically locked between the gear teeth, causing the paralysis. The main drive path is blocked by this complexity. Typography is a load-bearing structural element: Large, bold, blocky sans-serif Cyrillic across the top and bottom: 'ИЗЛИШНЯЯ СЛОЖНОСТЬ' (Overfitting) at the top, and 'ПАРАЛИЗУЕТ ПРОИЗВОДСТВО!' (Paralyzes Production!) at the bottom, both with heavy woodgrain and ink-bleed texture. Integrated small labels like 'КРИВАЯ ПЕРЕОБУЧЕНИЯ' (Overfitting Curve) and 'ВНИМАНИЕ ШУМУ!' (Attention to Noise!), and 'МОСКВА, 1925.'. The bottom right corner, instead of a chart, features an abstract, heavy geometric 'Proun' composition of intersecting red and black planes, where a jagged, data-like line representing the overfit path completely disrupts the geometric order, physically interacting with numbered iron data points.
tradition_approach: Adhered strictly to dynamic diagonal composition and a limited flat palette of red, black, and white. Used high-contrast black-and-white halftone photomontage texture for all machinery, explicitly omitting all modern 3D shading and digital smoothness. Typography (all Cyrillic, integrated) has heavy woodgrain and lithographic texture. The core metaphor fuses the concept directly into the machinery: a complex, paralyzing red wire (the overfit line) tries to connect to scattered, numbered outlier data points (iron plates/screws), causing physical mechanical stagnation.
